title.rendered: Zomato partner registration: How to tie up & add a restaurant

content.rendered:
Still wondering how to tie up with Zomato? If you have not yet registered with food ordering aggregators such as Zomato, then let us help you with the process. Zomato partner registration is not as difficult as you think it is. In fact, we are going to look at the whole process step by step so that you can right away register with Zomato as a partner and kick things into motion. Let’s get on with the Zomato restaurant registration process.

Step 1: Go to the official website of Zomato

The first thing you have to do is visit the official website and click on add a restaurant on Zomato. You can see the ‘Add Restaurant’ option on the top right corner of the website.

 After landing on the ‘Add Restaurant’ page, Click on the ‘Register your restaurant’ option. A pop-up will appear, where you will be asked to enter your name, email address, and mobile number. An OTP will be sent to your email id and after verification, you will be taken to the page with the registration form. 

Step 2: Fill the form with required details

Once the form appears, you can start entering all the necessary information that is asked. The first stage involves creating a page for your restaurant. In order to do so, you have to enter details like the name, address and accurate location of your restaurant. There will be a map provided, wherein you will have to place the pin correctly over the location of your restaurant. You have to then enter the details of the owner.

After doing so, the type of your restaurant and its timings have to be entered. Later on, you will be provided with a space to upload images of your restaurant including your current menu. 

Step 3: Some details that are to be kept handy

Before you start with the registration process, make sure to keep the following documents handy:

  • FSSAI Licence
  • GST number
  • PAN number
  • Shop Licence

Be serious when it comes to uploading these documents as Zomato double checks them and if the uploaded documents are not authentic, your registration process ends then and there. 

Step 4: Complete the registration process

Now that you have filled all the necessary information and submitted the form, you are good to go. Zomato will review your application and make your registration official if everything goes right. If the form-filling process seems too tedious to you, there is another option to get successfully registered with and become a Zomato restaurant partner. You can give a missed call on +91-8039654500 and someone from Zomato will get in touch with you within a day regarding the registration process. 

Step 5: Wait for the confirmation from Zomato

Once you have uploaded the necessary documents and every detail asked in the form is carefully filled and submitted, all you have to do is wait. Zomato will review your form and cross-check the information provided. The legal documents may be asked during the verification process also, so make sure to keep them by your side. You can use this time to come up with a Zomato partner online ordering menu, price of items, the additional charges, and so on. 

Step 6: You go live!

Within a few days, you will be contacted by a sales representative from Zomato to start getting the paperwork done and as a final step. You will be assigned a Point Of Contact. The POC is the person who will be the medium between you and Zomato. Then you officially go live. It’s as simple as that. 

Why should you register with Zomato: The benefits

If you are a restaurant owner and you have not yet registered with Zomato, it means that you have not yet opened your business to the vast potential of online food ordering culture. More and more people rely on the digital platform to get their food ordered and it is only natural that you throw your hat in the ring too. 

To put it in simple words:

  1. Registering with Zomato helps more people discover your restaurant 
  2. You can increase your sales and customer base exponentially
  3. Zomato takes care of your food delivery

Zomato Partner Registration Fee Structure

You may wonder if there is any registration fee associated with the whole process. As of now, there is no such thing and the process is completely free. But you do have to pay Zomato a commission of your sales, which usually amounts to somewhere between 15 to 22% and GST.

What to do next after Zomato registration?

  1. Always provide the best, right, and fresh food to your customer
  2. Give the standard quantity food
  3. Make sure the packing is good
  4. Try to get good reviews by following the above 3
  5. Use an easy to use restaurant order management system like Foaps. So you can manage multiple delivery platforms like Zomato, Swiggy and Dunzo in one place.

With Foaps, you can: 

  1. Manage orders
  2. Update menu in multiple platforms in a single update.
  3. Increase sales with the in-depth reports you get

Get started for free.

FAQs

How do I contact Zomato for a partnership?

Reachout to merchant@zomato.com or call at on +91-8039654500

Can I sell on Zomato from home?

Yes, you can.
